shared_with.note: Cyprus also drives on the left in the Mediterranean.
shared_with.diff: Limestone walls + triq → Malta; Greek script → Cyprus.

shared_with.note: Left-hand traffic + double yellow lines there too.

hints.text: Plates: blue strip left, but **short plates are very common** (unlike most of Europe)
Landscape: slightly hilly **dry agricultural fields**, 1–2-lane roads, cacti & dry shrubs (Mediterranean)
**Limestone rock walls** extremely common — this alone usually identifies Malta
Low buildings w/ **white-to-beige walls**, often limestone-built
Traditional **covered balconies**, often painted in bright colours
Street = **"triq"** — on almost every street sign
**Bus stops** very common — sometimes with useful location info
**Double yellow no-parking lines** along road edges

shared_with.note: UK uses the same double yellow lines.

shared_with.diff: Green lush landscape → Ireland; dry beige → Malta.

hints.text: **English** is the primary language; **Maltese** also everywhere — Latin script w/ unique letters **Ċ Ġ Ħ Ż**
hints.uniq_note: Ħ/ħ is the giveaway — no other Latin-script language uses the barred H.

hints.text: **Gozo Island**: metal guardrails rare — **low stone barriers** instead; all Gozo bus routes are in the **300s**
**Valletta**: these blue signs are unique to the capital
**Trekker on a white-blue ship** between the main island and Gozo
